2605.30219.title: When Should Models Change Their Minds? Contextual Belief Management in Large Language Models
2605.30219.paper_summary: Long-horizon interactions require language models to manage accumulating information: when to update their state, when to preserve their state, and what to ignore. We study this challenge as \textbf{Contextual Belief Management (CBM)}: maintaining a predicted belief state aligned with formal evidence while isolating task-irrelevant noise. To make CBM measurable, we introduce BeliefTrack, a closed-world benchmark spanning Rule Discovery and Circuit Diagnosis, where a finite belief space and symbolic verifiers enable exact turn-level evaluation. BeliefTrack diagnoses three failures: Failed Stay, Failed Update, and Failed Isolation. Across multiple LLMs, vanilla models exhibit severe CBM failures, while explicit belief-tracking prompts provide limited gains. In contrast, reinforcement learning with belief-state rewards reduces failure rates by 70.9\% on average. Further probing reveals latent belief-state dynamics behind these failures, and representation-level steering reduces failure rates by 46.1\% across two tasks\footnote{Code is coming soon at https://github.com/zjunlp/CBM.
2605.30219.paper_summary_zh: 長期互動要求語言模型管理累積的信息：何時更新其狀態，何時保留其狀態，以及忽略什麼。我們將這一挑戰研究為\textbf{上下文信念管理（CBM）}：在隔離與任務無關的噪音的同時，維持與正式證據一致的預測信念狀態。為了使CBM可測量，我們引入BeliefTrack，一個涵蓋規則發現和電路診斷的封閉世界基準，其中有限的信念空間和符號驗證器使得精確的回合級評估成為可能。BeliefTrack診斷出三種失敗：失敗保持、失敗更新和失敗隔離。在多個LLM中，普通模型顯示出嚴重的CBM失敗，而明確的信念追蹤提示提供的增益有限。相比之下，使用信念狀態獎勵的強化學習平均降低了70.9\%的失敗率。進一步探查揭示了這些失敗背後潛在的信念狀態動態，而表示層級的引導在兩個任務中將失敗率降低了46.1\%\footnote{代碼將很快在 https://github.com/zjunlp/CBM 上發布。}
2605.30219.author: Haoming Xu et.al.
2605.30219.authors: Haoming Xu, Weihong Xu, Zongrui Li, Mengru Wang, Yunzhi Yao, Chiyu Wu, Jin Shang, Yu Gong, Shumin Deng
